即使华盛顿成功阻止盟国和合作伙伴进口华为电信设备，但这也只是中国努力向这些国家挖洞的众多方式之一。经济和基础设施，造成依赖性，最终可能削弱这些合作伙伴。新的报告发现了美国的关系。这些国家中的许多甚至可能都不知道北京正在建立影响力的所有不同方式，更不用说应对它的计划了。
 国际战略研究所（IISS）在2月份的报告中研究了中国的“数字丝绸之路”战略，其中包括技术投资，共同开展研究的双边协议，用于资助学生学习中国技术，提供安全技术的资金。到专制政权等等。他们专门研究了印度尼西亚，韩国，阿拉伯联合酋长国，以色列和波兰，但问题几乎触及每个国家。
 他们发现，至少有16个国家与中国签署了与数字丝绸之路相关项目的谅解备忘录，这意味着两国已经就允许中国市场进入中国市场达成了某种形式的正式谅解。一个教育计划，或一起启动一项研究计划。但是，中国技术渗透的范围远远超出了这些正式协议。 IISS的研究表明，中国已经在DSR方面开展了相关项目，无论它在全球137个国家中赢得了市场立足点，教育地位还是其他方面。
 他们发现，许多国家的政府愿意在不同程度上与中国建立伙伴关系，但前提是不断变化的政治风向（以及不断变化的美国领导人）将导致美国放弃其敦促各国放弃中国技术的压力运动。但是他们发现这种对冲策略并没有影响该国与美国的国防和情报合作。
 报告说：“所分析的任何政府排除或限制中国技术整合的决定，纯粹是基于不这样做对与美国和盟国的国防和情报合作的假设后果。”
 该报告指出，在印度尼西亚，过去二十年来，华为“深深地，甚至是密不可分地”嵌入到该国的信息通信生态系统中，“从数千公里长的光纤电缆网络到最新的智能手机。中国设计的本地化应用程序在印尼智能手机用户中普遍存在，其通信通过中国设计的基站技术和数据中心进行传输和中继。印尼的大部分云显然是中国人。”中国在印尼的AI研究工作中也发挥着重要作用。
 在韩国，中国利用外国直接投资作为影响政策的“软硬兼施”工具。他们写道，韩国对中国的高科技进口“已成为中美硅芯片战争的中心舞台”，指的是美国和中国芯片制造商之间争夺更多电子产品的竞争。这表明“韩国政府显然会为韩美关系付出某种代价捍卫的一种错综复杂，复杂而利润丰厚的国家资产。” 
华为还给了中国在阿拉伯联合酋长国或阿联酋的重要立足点。但是，阿联酋政权购买监控摄像机和面部识别软件的关系加深，以警察其自身公民。
 在波兰，中国倾倒了培训和教育，如十几个波兰大学的奖项计划，其获奖者在深圳华为总部的一周内接受了一周的讲习班，第二周在北京和智能手机。他们发现华为去年6月刚刚与一个主要的波兰大学达成协议，这是重要的，因为它是在美国崛起的努力之后转向中国电信巨头的盟友，并在波兰努力保护盟友美国常驻美国基地失败了。
 即使是以色列，美国最近的安全伙伴之一占据了“中国数字丝绸之路的特殊地方”，签署了......与北京的研究和发展协议。以色列国防部，更多地对我们的担忧，一直在监测和提高自2000年代初以来中国活动的担忧。“
 这些协议和伙伴关系使中国不仅仅是市场上的立足点和政府政策制定的脚趾。他们还可以安全地访问中国的技术公司可以使用的民用和企业数据 - 并帮助北京的智力手术更有效地针对具有伪造的公民。
 “即使假设中国技术为智力安全引起的风险是低的，利用大数据的能力应该是防御行业的关注，因为它与机器学习和人工智能（AI）的未来竞争力有关，”他们写。
 IIS发现的最大问题是，政府不采取任何措施来确定中国投资有多少或有什么样的风险。 “辩论也集中在很大程度上是关于是否接受中国人的物理基础设施并没有，似乎依靠来自中国的铜线进口的辩论，或是否允许中国投资进入当地的初创行业。因此，似乎从这项研究中似乎很难精确确定中国ICT技术的融合程度应该被认为是重要的。“

Even if Washington  succeeds in keeping allies and partners from importing Huawei telecom gear, that's just one of the many ways China is working to burrow into these countries' economies and infrastructure, creating dependencies that may ultimately weaken these partners' U.S. ties, a  new report finds. And many of these countries may not even know all of the different ways Beijing is building influence, much less have a plan to confront it.
 The February report from the  International Institute for Strategic Studies, or IISS, looked at China’s “Digital Silk Road” strategy, which includes technology investments, bilateral agreements to conduct research together, funding for students to learn about Chinese tech, the provision of security tech to autocratic regimes, and more. They looked specifically at Indonesia, South Korea, the United Arab Emirates, Israel, and Poland but the issue touches nearly every country.
 They found that at least 16 countries had signed memorandums of understanding with with China on projects related to the Digital Silk Road, meaning that that the two countries had reached some sort of formal understanding about, say, allowing Chinese tech in the marketplace, China hosting an education program, or launching a research program together. But the scope of Chinese tech infiltration goes well beyond those formal agreements. IISS’s research showed that China had carried out projects related to the DSR, be it gaining market foothold, education, etc. in 137 countries worldwide.
 They found many governments were willing, to various extents, to entertain partnerships with China under the assumption that changing political winds (and changing U.S. leadership) would lead the United States to abandon  its pressure campaign to urge countries to eschew Chinese technology. But they found that this hedging strategy didn’t affect the countries’ defense and intelligence cooperation with the United States.
 “The decision to exclude or limit the integration of Chinese technology by any of the governments analysed was based purely on the hypothetical consequences of not doing so for defence and intelligence cooperation with the US and allies,” the report said.
 In Indonesia, the report noted, the past two decades have seen Huawei become “deeply, if not inextricably, embedded” in the country’s information communications ecosystem, “from fibre-optic cable networks thousands of kilometres long to the latest smartphones. Chinese-designed localised apps are prevalent among Indonesian smartphone users, whose communications are transmitted and relayed by Chinese-designed base-station technology and data centres. Much of the Indonesian cloud is apparently Chinese.” China is also playing a big role in Indonesia’s AI research efforts.
 In South Korea, China uses foreign direct investment as a “carrot-and-stick tool” to influence policy. South Korean tech imports to China have “been centre stage in the Sino-US silicon-chip war,” they write, referring to the  jockeying between U.S. and Chinese chip makers to get their chips in more electronics. That reveals “an intricate, complex and enormously lucrative national asset which the Korean establishment will apparently defend at some cost to ROK–US relations.”
 Huawei has also given China an important foothold in the United Arab Emirates, or UAE. But the relationship deepened by the UAE regime’s purchases of surveillance cameras and facial recognition software to police its own citizens.
 In Poland, China poured money into training and education, such as prize programs at a dozen Polish universities whose winners received a week of workshops at Huawei headquarters in Shenzhen, a second week in Beijing, and a smartphone. They found that Huawei had an agreement with a major Polish university just last June, which is significant because it comes after the United States had ramped up efforts to turn allies away from the Chinese telecom giant and around the same time that Polish efforts to  secure a permanent U.S. base in the country failed.
 Even Israel, one of the United States’ closest security partners, occupies “a special place along China’s Digital Silk Road,” having signed a…  research and development agreement with Beijing. The Israeli Ministry of Defense, more attuned to US concerns, has been monitoring and raising concerns about China’s activities since the early 2000s.”
 Those agreements and partnerships give China more than a foothold in markets and a toe in government policymaking. They also secure access to civilian and corporate data that can be of use to China’s tech companies — and help Beijing’s intelligence operatives  more effectively target citizens with disinformation.
 “Even if it is assumed that the risk posed by Chinese technologies to intelligence security is low, the ability to harness big data should be of concern to defence industries as it has relevance to future competitiveness in machine learning and artificial intelligence (AI),” they write.
 The biggest problem, IISS found, is that governments are taking no steps to determine how much or what kind of Chinese investment posed a possible risk. “Debates also focused largely on whether to accept toplevel Chinese physical infrastructure and did not, for example, seem to delve into debates around whether to rely on imports of copper wire from China, or whether to permit Chinese investment into local start-up industries. It would thus seem from this research that it is difficult for national-level governments to precisely determine what level of integration of Chinese ICT technologies should be considered significant.”

title: 报告详细介绍了中国如何利用科技产品，市场立足点和培训来建立对印度尼西亚，韩国，阿联酋，以色列和波兰的依赖
title_en: Report details how China is using tech products, market foothold, and training, to create dependencies in Indonesia, South Korea, UAE, Israel, and Poland

note:
微软和FireEye的高管周二敦促国会在联邦政府大规模入侵俄罗斯之后，对公司制定强制性违规报告要求。
 微软总裁布拉德·史密斯（Brad Smith）在参议院情报委员会的书面证词中说：“我们需要对私营部门施加明确，一致的披露义务。”他指出，当公司被黑客入侵时，“沉默就会盛行”。
 史密斯说：“这是使严重问题变得更糟的秘诀，它要求我们所有人都做出改变。” “我们需要以明确，一贯的义务来代替这种沉默，让私营部门组织在受到确认的重大事件影响时公开信息。”
  FireEye首席执行官凯文·曼迪亚（Kevin Mandia）曾因对所谓的SolarWinds违规事件的早期了解而受到赞誉，他说，公司应该有一种方法可以报告违规行为，并可能对国家安全造成重大影响，而不必担心法律制裁。
 曼迪亚在书面证词中说：“美国政府应该考虑一项联邦公开计划，不仅要共享威胁指标，而且还应提供违规或事件的通知。”
 SolarWinds漏洞的发现很大程度上归因于FireEye的披露，即12月，一个复杂的黑客组织已对其进行了入侵。黑客利用IT组织SolarWinds中的软件漏洞来破坏多达18,000个SolarWinds客户，其中包括FireEye和Microsoft。
 一位白宫官员上周表示，在长达数月的行动中，已经确认有9个联邦机构和100个私营部门实体受到了损害，美国情报官员称这起源于俄罗斯。 
尽管周二没有一个行业领导人明确表示俄罗斯是这次袭击的幕后黑手，但在许多人看来这已经成定局了。
  史密斯说：“在现阶段，我们已经找到了指向俄罗斯外国情报局的大量证据，而没有发现任何证据可以将我们引向其他任何地方。” “就我们在谈论谁而言，目前没有太多悬念。”
 曼迪亚（Mandia）和史密斯（Smith）还表示，法律上没有要求其公司公开披露其遭到违反。
 “我们没有举报的法律义务，但是我认为我们仍然有义务：首先对每个客户；其次是美国政府史密斯证明。 “没有这种共享，我们将不会确保这个国家的安全。”
 美国大多数州在账簿上都有某种形式的受害者违规通知要求，但是尽管经过多年的努力，联邦政府仍未能将受害者违规通知落实到位。结果，SolarWinds违规的全部范围尚未明确，因为许多受损害的公司选择不提出要求，从而可能进一步危害客户。
 参议院情报委员会主席Mark Warner Microsoft，FireEye在SolarWinds骇客听证会后敦促制定违规报告规则：“我们完全在等待有意愿的参与者，因为其他大型企业也可能成为受害者，但我们并没有选择挺身而出。调查大规模的SolarWinds违反事件的后果参议院情报小组将于下周举行关于SolarWinds违反事件的听证会。更多（D-Va。）周二表示。
  华纳敦促在听证会中多次提出违反通知的要求，特别是因为许多公司保持沉默。 
“有越来越多的思想是，我们需要一定程度的信息至少在强制性地分享的基础上，”华纳表示，建议在联邦一级创造一个“新手”，以引领这一努力。
 他强调，虽然他可能是“开放”对报告违约的公司的一些责任保护，但他不支持任何借口“滑雪性行为”的保护。
 “我们将需要考虑一个不同的模式，我挑战你们所有人都提出来，”华纳告诉证人。 “我认为有很多两性的肉体胃口。我想我们意识到我们有多严重，我们可能躲避更严重的子弹。“
  “我们从白宫的报告和我们自己的简报中知道，黑客确实获得了至少九个联邦机构网络，但美国政府通过Fireeye了解到这项攻击，”Susan Collins Microsoft，Fireeye推动违约Solarwinds Hack上的钱之后的规则：舒默敦促民主党人在一起.9T账单COLLINS规定了拜登救济计划的GOP支持鲍威尔斗争通胀担心舒默敦促民主党人粘在一起.9t账单更多（缅因州）说。
  “应该有这种信息交流现在在任何一方都没有发生，”她在政府与私营部门之间分享威胁情报。
 Mandia强调，虽然SolarwindsBreac区被发现并停止，但不可避免地发生另一个类似的违规行为，突出了对违反违反通知要求的需求。
 “这个攻击者，也许他们的铅笔掉了几个月，但现实是他们要回来，”Mandia说。 “他们如何闯入总是不断发展，我们所能做的就是关闭窗户，下次更好地关闭安全差距。”

Top executives from Microsoft and FireEye on Tuesday urged Congress to create mandatory breach reporting requirements for companies following the massive Russian hack of the federal government that extended to the private sector.
 “We need to impose a clear, consistent disclosure obligation on the private sector,” Microsoft President  Brad Smith said in written testimony to the Senate Intelligence Committee, noting that “silence reigns” when companies are hacked.
 “This is a recipe for making a formidable problem even worse, and it requires all of us to change,” Smith said. “We need to replace this silence with a clear, consistent obligation for private sector organizations to disclose when they’re impacted by confirmed significant incidents.”
  FireEye CEO Kevin Mandia, whose company was credited with shining an early light on what has become known as the SolarWinds breach, said there should be a way for companies to report breaches with potential national security ramifications without fear of legal retribution.
 “The U.S. government should consider a federal disclosure program for not only sharing threat indicators but for also providing notification of a breach or incident,” Mandia said in written testimony.
 The discovery of the SolarWinds breach was due in large part to FireEye’s disclosure that it had been breached by a sophisticated hacking group in December. The hackers used vulnerabilities in software from IT group SolarWinds to compromise up to 18,000 customers of SolarWinds, with FireEye and Microsoft among those breached.
 A White House official said last week that nine federal agencies and 100 private sector entities were confirmed to have been compromised in the months-long operation, which U.S. intelligence officials have described as “likely” Russian in origin.
 While none of the industry leaders Tuesday would say definitively that Russia was behind the attack, it seemed to many a foregone conclusion.
  “At this stage we have found substantial evidence that points to the Russian foreign intelligence agency, and we have found no evidence that leads us anywhere else,” Smith said. “There is not a lot of suspense at this moment in terms of who we are talking about.”
 Mandia and Smith also said that their companies were not legally required to publicly disclose that they had been breached.
 “We had no legal obligation to report, but I think we had a duty nonetheless: first of all to each customer; secondly to the U.S. government; and third of all to the public,” Smith testified. “We will not secure this country without that kind of sharing.”
 Most U.S. states have some form of victim breach notification requirements on the books, but the federal government has failed to put one in place despite years of efforts. As a result, the full scope of the SolarWinds breach has not yet become clear, as many companies compromised have chosen not to come forward, potentially further endangering customers.
 “We are totally waiting on willing participants, we could still be uninformed because other major enterprises could be victims as well but have not chosen to come forward,” Senate Intelligence Committee Chairman   Mark Warner      Microsoft, FireEye push for breach reporting rules after SolarWinds hack  Hearings examine consequences of massive SolarWinds breach  Senate Intelligence panel to hold hearing on SolarWinds breach next week  MORE  (D-Va.) said Tuesday.
  Warner pressed for putting in place breach notification requirements multiple times during the hearing, particularly as many companies remain silent.
 “There is a growing sense that we need some level of at least information sharing on a mandatory basis,” Warner said, suggesting the creation of a “new player” at the federal level to lead this effort.
 He stressed that while he may be “open” to some liability protections for companies that report breaches, he would not support any protections excusing “sloppy behavior.”
 “We’re going to need to think about a different model, and I challenge all of you to come forward with that,” Warner told the witnesses. “I think there is a great deal of bipartisan appetite. I think we realize how serious we were and we potentially dodged a much more serious bullet.”
  “We know from the White House’s report and our own briefings that the hackers did gain access to at least nine federal agency networks, yet the United States government learned of this attack through FireEye,” Sen.   Susan Collins      Microsoft, FireEye push for breach reporting rules after SolarWinds hack  On The Money: Schumer urges Democrats to stick together on .9T bill | Collins rules out GOP support for Biden relief plan | Powell fights inflation fears  Schumer urges Democrats to stick together on .9T bill  MORE  (Maine) said.
  “There should be this exchange of information that is not occurring now on either side,” she noted in regards to sharing of threat intelligence between the government and private sector.
 Mandia underlined that while the SolarWinds breach had been discovered and halted, inevitably another similar breach would occur, highlighting the need for tougher breach notification requirements.
 “This attacker, maybe their pencil is down for a few months, but the reality is they are going to come back,” Mandia said. “How they break in is always evolving, and all we can do is close the window and close the security gap better next time.”

Emeryville, California-based industrial robotics startup  Symbio Robotics today emerged from stealth with $30 million in funding. The company says the capital will be put toward further developing its technology as it looks to sign new customers.
 McKinsey  pegs the automation potential for production occupations at 79%, and the pandemic is likely to accelerate this shift. A  report by the Manufacturing Institute and Deloitte found that 4.6 million manufacturing jobs will need to be filled over the next decade, and challenges brought on by physical distancing measures and a sustained uptick in ecommerce activity have stretched some logistics operations to the limit.
  Symbio, which was founded in 2014, claims its software can help by allowing factory-floor robots to “learn” what to accomplish. The company’s middleware lets developers use modern programming languages like Java, Ruby, and Python to create instructions for single or whole fleets of robots. The platform self-optimizes over time, letting robots improve on tasks they’ve been assigned.
 From subassembly to final assembly, Symbio’s software supports automation that can adapt to variations in processes. Using AI and feedback from automation sensors, Symbio can help a robot to recognize and pick bins containing randomly distributed parts, or adjust to things like unique fastener positions and orientations. Moreover, Symbio can enable robots with cameras to track moving parts while executing high-precision tasks.
 Symbio’s software runs on edge computing infrastructure through industrial networks to inform and instruct factory systems to execute assembly tasks. As opposed to fully automating pipelines, the company says it’s focused on building automation that supports “the best of human-machine collaboration.”
 “Symbio is doing for manufacturing what Windows did for DOS. Existing industrial manufacturing robots run proprietary programming languages making them slow and cumbersome. Not only do these robots lack dexterity, they lack the intelligence to make them do what they need to do,” cofounder and CEO Max Reynolds said in a press release. “Our technology is designed to fundamentally reframe these existing manufacturing pain points by utilizing the best practices of AI and human robot interaction. That’s what we believe will drive success.”
 Symbio says it’s already working with Nissan and Toyota in addition to other automotive companies. The recent investment was led by ACME Capital with participation from existing investors including Andreessen Horowitz, Eclipse Ventures, and The House Fund.
 “We’ve seen cars become much more complex and customized in the decades since the onset of industrial automation. Today’s automakers need more nimble and nuanced robotics to perform increasingly sophisticated assemblies,” Eclipse Ventures partner Greg Reichow, the former head of global manufacturing and automation engineering at Tesla, added. “Symbio provides the exact solution for forward-thinking manufacturers, through an AI-powered platform that combines advanced compute, sensing, and precision-control software.”
 Symbio’s fundraising follows a decade of multimillion-dollar bets on warehouse, logistics, and assembly automation. Amazon acquired robotics company Kiva Systems for $775 million in March 2012, and in November 2019, DHL  announced it would invest $300 million to modernize its warehouses in North America with IoT sensors and robots. Separately, startups like  Attabotics and  CommonSense Robotics have raised tens of millions of dollars for compact automated fulfillment centers that can slot into tight spaces. Others like  Covariant and  Soft Robotics have won over investors with control systems that can pick, place, and unload objects in warehouses.

“The reason the channel performs so well is it’s not saturated,” Zatulove said. But that’s changing, and as it does, companies will have to do more to “cut through the noise.”
 That’s what he said Emotive provides, by enabling text marketing that feels like a real conversation with another human being, rather than just another email blast. He compared it to the sales associate who would greet you when you first walked into a department store, pre-COVID.
  “The online sales associate really didn’t exist,” he said. “That’s what we’re trying to provide.”
 Emotive saw 466% year-over-year revenue growth in 2020 and is announcing today that it has raised $50 million in a Series B funding round that values the company at $400 million. It was led by CRV with participation from Mucker Capital, TenOneTen Ventures and Stripes.
  “Never underestimate the importance of building a product that your customers, and your customers’ customers adore,” said CRV general partner Murat Bicer in a statement. “One of the things that struck us about Emotive is the sheer amount of customer love Brian and Zack get from meal delivery services, manufacturing companies and even toddler shoe brands. Small businesses find it easy to set up campaigns and their customers genuinely prefer communicating with someone over text rather than email.”
 Zatulove said he founded the company with Zachary Wise after they’d worked together on cannabis loyalty startup Reefer, eventually deciding there was a bigger opportunity here after their early successes with text marketing. He explained that while Emotive works with larger customers, its sweet spot is mid-sized e-commerce businesses on Shopify, Magento, Bigcommerce and Woocommerce.
 Since those businesses usually don’t have any salespeople of their own yet, Emotive serves that function. It can start conversations around shopping cart abandonment and promote promote sales and new products, resulting in what the company says are 8% to 10% conversion rates (compared to 1% or 2% for a standard text marketing campaign). Zatuolove said the platform largely relied on human responders at first, and although it’s become increasingly automated over time, Emotive still has an internal team handling responses when necessary.
 “We never plan on losing that human touch as part of the dialogue,” he added. “We see ourselves as a human-to-human platform. That’s our biggest differentiator.”
 Emotive had previously raised $8.2 million in funding,  according to Crunchbase. Zatulove said this new round will allow the company to continue developing the product, to grow its headcount to more than 200 people and to open offices in Atlanta and Boston. Eventually, it could also expand beyond texting.
 “Longer term, we see ourselves more as a conversation platform, not just as a text message platform,” he said.

 因此，BitFineX和Tether将不再允许与纽约市民有任何交易活动。公司还需要定期提供有关业务职能的报告，提供有关其作为储备金的资金的明确细节。系绳必须提供资产支持系绳的公开披露，按类别分开，包括附属实体的任何贷款。
   在一份声明中，Tether表示，它没有在结算条款下承认任何不法行为。 
＆＃34;总检察长办公室得出结论，从本质上讲，我们可以更好地公开披露这些事件。 与在线投机相反，在两年半之后，没有发现Tether发行过没有支持或操纵加密货币价格的Tether，＆＃34; 系绳说。  没有发现Tether发行时没有支持或影响加密价格的发现。 这项和解表明了我们对行业未来的承诺，以及对透明性的承诺，未来将按季度披露@tether_to储备金。 2/3  Tether承认确实使NYAG强调了这笔贷款，但表示此后已全额偿还。 Tether说，这笔贷款并没有阻止该公司在任何时候发出赎回。  为了获得最佳体验，请立即下载顶级加密新闻和专有功能。
note_en:
The NYAG is requiring that Bitfinex and Tether stop operating in New York, according to a statement.
    Bitfinex and   Tether have agreed to stop all trading activity in the state of New York as part of a settlement  announced today by New York Attorney General Letitia James. The two firms also agreed to pay $18.5 million in fines to end the state’s 22-month investigation into their activities.
 “Bitfinex and Tether recklessly and unlawfully covered-up massive financial losses to keep their scheme going and protect their bottom lines,” said Attorney General James. “Tether’s claims that its virtual currency was fully backed by U.S. dollars at all times was a lie. These companies obscured the true risk investors faced and were operated by unlicensed and unregulated individuals and entities dealing in the darkest corners of the financial system."
  According to the statement, the two firms made false statements about the backing of Tether and the movement of hundreds of millions of dollars between the company—to cover up losses when its shadow bank Crypto Capital had its funds confiscated by police.
 The statement referenced the OAG investigation, which it says found that from mid-2017, Tether had no access to the banking system and did not have reserves to back the Tether in circulation at the time.
 As a result, Bitfinex and Tether will no longer be allowed to have any trading activity with citizens of New York. The companies will also need to provide regular reports on business functions, providing clear details on their funds held as reserves. Tether must provide public disclosures of the assets backing Tether, separated by categories, including any loans to or from affiliated entities.
   In a  statement, Tether said it has not admitted any wrongdoing under the terms of the settlement.
 "The Attorney General’s Office concluded, in essence, that we could have done better in publicly disclosing these events. Contrary to online speculation, after two and half years there was no finding that Tether ever issued tethers without backing, or to manipulate crypto prices," Tether said.
 No finding that Tether ever issued without backing or to impact crypto prices. This settlement shows our commitment to the future of the industry, and to transparency with quarterly disclosures of  @tether_to reserves going forward. 2/3
  Tether acknowledged that it did make the loan highlighted by the NYAG, but said it has since been repaid in full. Tether said that the loan did not prevent the firm from issuing redemptions at any time.

PlayStation 5将拥有自己的虚拟现实耳机，但是，消费者可能面临持续的困难获得PS5控制台给出供应链短缺。 PlayStation首席执行官Jim Ryan在周一访谈中透露了华盛顿邮政的两种发展。
  Ryan表示，PS5特定的VR耳机的发展套件将很快发出，尽管该公司还没有准备好谈论设备的马力或规格。他确实说，下一个耳机将比当前的PSVR设置相反，需要通过PlayStation 4，电视和名为PSVR处理器的单独黑匣子运行的电线。
 “世代跨越子允许您扫除已经发生的技术的进步，”Ryan说。 “鉴于这是我们第一次进入虚拟现实，它让我们有机会应用经验教训。其中一个非常生动的插图，是我们将移动到一个非常简单的单线设置。“
   PlayStation VR的下一个版本也将从其突破性的双静脉控制器借用，该控制器与PS5首次亮相，并从游戏中提供超特定的触觉反馈到玩家手的手掌。
 “我们兴奋的创新之一是我们的新VR控制器，它将纳入DualSense无线控制器中发现的一些主要功能，并侧重于伟大的人体工程学，”高级副总裁，平台规划＆amp; Management Hideaki Nishino在PlayStation的网站周二写道上写道。
 Ryan的说法，新的VR设备没有设置的发布日期。 2020年10月20日在帖子采访时，Ryan表示，索尼对VR仍然非常感兴趣，任何关于公司VR投资的新闻可能不会进入2021年。
  “我认为我们从VR的未来超过了几分钟，”Ryan说那么。 “Playstation相信VR。索尼相信VR，我们肯定相信未来的某个观点，VR将代表一个有意义的互动娱乐组成部分。这是今年吗？不，这是明年吗？不，但它会出现在某个阶段？我们相信。我们非常满意，我们对PlayStation VR获得的所有经验，我们期待看到未来带来的地方。“ 
索尼VR平台的延续确保了PlayStation 5至少可以证明流行的虚拟现实游戏的未来可能。 Facebook在2014年以约20亿美元的价格购买了Oculus平台，由于该设备的可访问性，它一直在稳步增加市场份额，并于2020年秋季推出了改进且更便宜的Oculus Quest2。去年，Valve发布了“半条命Alyx， ”被广泛认为是虚拟现实故事讲述中的里程碑。虽然不那么受欢迎，但索尼的第一方VR头衔“ Astro Bot Rescue Mission”被誉为有史以来最具创新性的平台游戏之一。
  但是，消费者对PlayStation 5的可访问性仍然是一个持续的问题。赖安说，索尼“很抱歉”，尽管到12月底为止已经售出了450万台游戏机，但仍有更多人无法购买PlayStation 5游戏机。 Ryan说，在整个大流行期间，索尼机器以及微软Xbox产品线的控制台短缺一直很稀缺，这继续影响着供应链。
 赖安说，由于大流行开始以来消费电子产品的需求，索尼绝对受到当前全球半导体芯片短缺的影响。该公司发现自己在确保更多处理器方面与汽车制造商和智能手机公司竞争。尽管如此，索尼仍然能够实现Ryan的期望，即他们的新机器的销量将比2013年底之前售出的PlayStation 4游戏机（420万台）更多。
  “需求比我们预期的要大，”瑞安说。 “那，加上供应链问题的复杂性，导致供应量略低于我们最初的预期。”
   Ryan表示PS5的生产“正在稳步提高”。他希望随着零售商店的开业，人们可以摆脱僵尸网络的在线零售体验。当特制的软件程序正在帮助将在线出售的游戏机传送给购买者，以打算在二手市场上转售它们时，可能成为PS5的购买者正在遇到一个问题。
  Ryan也为PlayStation Network的新注册量感到兴奋。 Ryan说，四分之五的PS5拥有者从未拥有过PlayStation 4，这意味着索尼正在收购或欢迎新客户或老客户，其中约有一半是PSN的新用户。
 而且由于锁定仍在进行中，因此PlayStation将通过赠送字幕标题来继续其“在家娱乐”活动。去年该程序启动时，“神秘海域：内森·德雷克收藏集”被下载了1000万次。即将推出，玩家可以期待2016年发布的“ Ratchet and Clank”，这将为6月发布的PS5独家“ Ratchet and Clank：Rift Apart”带来兴奋。
note_en:
The PlayStation 5 will have its own virtual reality headset, however, consumers may face ongoing difficulties obtaining a PS5 console given a supply chain shortfall. PlayStation CEO Jim Ryan revealed both developments in a Monday interview with The Washington Post.
  Ryan said development kits for the PS5-specific VR headset will be sent out soon, though the company isn’t ready to talk about the device’s horsepower or specs. He did say the next headset will be considerably less cumbersome, as opposed to the current PSVR setup that requires wires running through a PlayStation 4, the TV and a separate black box called the PSVR processor.
 “Generational leaps allows you to sweep up the advances in technology that have taken place,” Ryan said. “Given this was our first foray into virtual reality, it gives us a chance to apply lessons learned. One of the very vivid illustrations of that is that we will be moving to a very easy single-cord setup.”
   The next version of PlayStation VR will also borrow from its groundbreaking DualSense controllers, which debuted with the PS5 and provide super specific haptic feedback from the game to the palms of a player’s hands.
 “One of the innovations we’re excited about is our new VR controller, which will incorporate some of the key features found in the DualSense wireless controller, along with a focus on great ergonomics,” Senior Vice President, Platform Planning & Management Hideaki Nishino wrote in a post on PlayStation’s website Tuesday.
 There’s no set launch date for the new VR device, according to Ryan. In an  October 2020 interview with The Post, Ryan said while Sony was still very much interested in VR, any more news about the company’s VR investments may not come in 2021.
  “I think we’re more than a few minutes from the future of VR,” Ryan said then. “PlayStation believes in VR. Sony believes in VR, and we definitely believe at some point in the future, VR will represent a meaningful component of interactive entertainment. Will it be this year? No. Will it be next year? No. But will it come at some stage? We believe that. And we’re very pleased with all the experience that we’ve gained with PlayStation VR, and we look forwarding to seeing where that takes us in the future.”
   The continuation of Sony’s VR platform ensures that the PlayStation 5 is at least proofed for any possible future of popular virtual reality titles. Facebook paid about $2 billion to purchase the Oculus platform in 2014, and has steadily been increasing marketshare thanks to the device’s accessibility, introducing the improved and cheaper Oculus Quest 2 in the Fall of 2020. Last year, Valve released “Half-Life Alyx,” widely considered a milestone in virtual reality storytelling. While not as popular, Sony’s first-party VR title “Astro Bot Rescue Mission” was hailed as one of the most innovative platformers ever created.
  The accessibility of the PlayStation 5 to consumers remains an ongoing issue, however. Ryan said Sony is “very sorry” that more people haven’t been able to buy PlayStation 5 consoles, despite having sold 4.5 million machines through the end of December. Console shortages for Sony’s machine, as well as Microsoft’s Xbox line have been scarce during the entire pandemic, which  continues to affect the supply chain, Ryan said.
 Ryan said Sony is absolutely affected by the  current global semiconductor chip shortage, due to the demand of consumer electronics since the pandemic began. The company found itself competing with auto manufacturers and smartphone companies on securing more processors. Despite that, Sony was still able to deliver on Ryan’s expectation that they would sell more of the new machine than PlayStation 4 consoles sold through the end of 2013 (4.2 million).
  “Demand was greater than we anticipated,” Ryan said. “That, along with the complexities of the supply chain issues, resulted in a slightly lower supply than we initially anticipated.”
   Ryan said production of PS5s is “ramping up steadily.” He hopes that as retail stores open up, people can be rid of the bot-swarmed online retail experience. Would-be PS5 buyers have had ongoing issues securing one when specially crafted software programs are helping to shuttle consoles sold online to purchasers intent on re-selling them for a profit on the secondary market.
  Ryan has also been warmed by the amount of new sign-ups to the PlayStation Network. One out of four PS5 owners never owned a PlayStation 4, said Ryan, which means Sony is acquiring or welcoming back new or lapsed customers, and that about half of them are new to PSN.
 And because lockdown is still ongoing, PlayStation will continue its “Play At Home” campaign by giving away marquee titles. When the program launched last year, “Uncharted: The Nathan Drake Collection” was downloaded 10 million times. Coming soon, players can expect the 2016 release of “Ratchet and Clank,” to build excitement for the PS5 exclusive “Ratchet and Clank: Rift Apart,” releasing in June.

另一个杰夫正在从他的长期亚马逊执行角色开始，填写了在科技巨头的大规模领导过渡的照片。
 杰夫布朗本，高级副总裁们在杰夫贝罗斯的顶级中尉之一播放了一个关键的幕后角色超过二十几年，是亚马逊，截至今天，他在一年悠久的休假之后，他在一条消息中写道到亚马逊的高级领导人，发布到公司内联网并由Geekwire获得。
 “我不是退休，并将有关于我很快的内容，”布莱克本写道。
  该公司不言而喻，布莱克本预计接下来将降落，但贝奥斯明确表示，这不太可能是争论的主题。
 Blackburn“让我在下一章的循环中，我可以向你保证这是一个非常令人兴奋的！” Bezos在他的回答中写道，也在周二发布到亚马逊内联网。 “我预测，杰夫在他在亚马逊的所有角色都是他的新角色，杰夫会令人惊讶。轻松预测。祝你恭喜你，杰夫。“ [查看下面的Bezos和Blackburn的完整消息。]
 亚马逊的行政套房距离亚马逊的行政套房有很多。
 Bezos本月宣布计划在今年第三季度担任首席执行官，转向作为执行主席的角色，而是由Andy Jassy的首席执行官替换为长期的亚马逊网络服务领导者。 
亚马逊全球消费者首席执行官杰夫·威尔克（Jeff Wilke）于10月宣布，他将于今年第一季度辞职，由前全球业务主管戴夫·克拉克（Dave Clark）接任。
 布莱克本在给高级领导团队或S团队的信息中表现出幽默感，开玩笑说他的离职将解决的一个挑战。
  “已经困扰了很多人二十年的'太多杰夫'错误已经修复！”他写了。 “这些年来，我们一直有多个Jeff在同一个团队中-Jeff Wilke，Jeff B，我本人以及一段时间内Jeff Holden。实际上，这就是我成为你们中许多人和整个亚马逊员工的“黑人”的原因。威尔克（Wilke）的退休意味着少了杰夫（Jeff），而今天，我将其降至原来的水平。”
 像威尔克一样，布莱克本以前被视为贝佐斯（Bezos）首席执行官的潜在继任者，不是因为名字的连续性，而是因为经验的丰富。正如布莱克本在备忘录中指出的那样，他与亚马逊的关系始于他在德意志银行担任初级投资银行家时，被分配从事亚马逊IPO的工作。在他超过21年的积极工作期间，Blackburn领导了包括亚马逊的第三方销售，Prime Video，Amazon Music和Amazon Advertising在内的关键业务，并与Bezos一起开展了关键业务计划。
 布莱克本在星期二的致辞中回顾了一个关键时刻：“在1998年初，传奇的首席财务官乔伊·科维（Joy Covey）告诉我，该网站可能不仅仅只是书籍……‘你现在就应该来到这里，相信我’。哇，您的意思是像Tapes＆amp;光盘和VHS？完毕。”
  他还一路应对公共挑战。在2018年，布莱克本（Blackburn）遭到亚马逊影业公司员工的批评，据称他们认为他对工作室负责人罗伊·普赖斯（Roy Price）在对普赖斯的性骚扰指控后辞职的情况蒙上了一层阴影。布莱克本（Blackburn）与员工交谈时，“希望为已经采取大胆的新战略的组织提供新的开始”，但他的信息“使一些员工感到沮丧”，当时新闻媒体《信息》报道。
  布莱克本（Blackburn）离开的一个影响是，给贾西（Jassy）一个重塑亚马逊领导团队的机会，因为他接任今年晚些时候担任首席执行官。该公司尚未发布的重要公告是，谁将接替Jassy担任该公司快速发展且盈利的云计算部门AWS的首席执行官。 
在他对领导团队的信息中，布莱克本说他希望他推动他们和亚马逊“更加大胆，看到围绕角落，想想大，”在他的任期里。 “与核心业务一样强大的职位，因为它是迄今为止的，我知道这就是我们在未来十年内从你那里想要的东西。”
 这是Blackburn的消息，其次是Bezos的回复，它发布到亚马逊的Intranet。
 Steam：我对大家有一个重要的更新，我知道你一直在等待。 “太多的杰夫的”虫子已经困扰了许多几十年的困扰！多年来，我们总是在S-Team-Jeff Wilke，杰夫B，我自己以及一段时间杰夫·霍尔登上有多个杰夫。实际上，我是如何对亚马逊的许多人和员工变成“jblack”。 Wilke的退休意味着杰夫，今天，我把它带到原来。
 我今天正式离开亚马逊。 1996年底，我是Deutsche Bank的初级投资银行家，分配给潜在的亚马逊IPO项目。我做了Excel模型，写了一个amzn的Ipo招股说明书的块等。这一天，这一天的东西粘在一起，我是在我们三周的全球旅行中运载所有团队较重的袋子的东西。没有缩放或钟声，所以它是一个艰苦的过程。
 1998年初，我被传奇的CFO Joy Covey告诉我，该网站可能会变得不仅仅是书......“你现在需要在这里出现，相信我”。哇，你的意思是像磁带＆amp; CDS＆amp; VHS？完毕。
 我此后很快就加入了亚马逊。我会和你一起做大量建筑 -  3P市场，M＆amp; A和Busdev团队，A9，广告，亚马逊音乐，Prime Videa，Amazon Studios。在我的22年里，我想认为我从未停止帮助携带那些较重的袋子。更新的举措，我们团队采取了额外勇气和长期思考的举措。我希望，在边缘，我推动了S-Team和亚马逊更加大胆，看到了角落，想大。随着它的核心业务，它是迄今为止的强大的一个位置，我知道在未来十年内客户将从您身上想要的。
 Gotrifurif的新S-Team领导者已经进入了这些大角色，并继续建立和大胆发明。我欣赏过去11个月的领导力：观看公司在Covid期间执行的令人惊讶 - 你的团队在最需要的时候提供亚马逊的最佳状态。 
我要感谢你们所有人的友谊，团队合作和支持。它在一起的惊人22年 - 我会永远珍惜它。我不是退休，并将有关于我很快的内容。
 当然，今天，我正式放弃“jblack @”。我只有一个要求 - 我在亚马逊大厅里永远留下“jblack”。这是一个荣誉，
 当你被分配到IPO路演时，这对我们来说是一个幸运的日子。看着你作为那个初级投资银行家，很容易看到你的邪恶智慧 - 完全展示。但是，谁知道你会被发明和先锋的无情的冲动所驱动？从市场和广告到推出Prime Video和Amazon Studios，你是一个建设者。而且你已经帮助了我们所有人 - 不仅仅是你直接领导的东西。你有判断力，你的观点永远不会冗余。当你说话时，我发现自己仔细倾向于倾听 - 永远。
 谢谢你超过二十年的领导力。推动我们所有人继续探索。携带所有这些袋子。
 虽然它没有完全准备被分享，但Jblack让我在下一章的循环中，我可以向你保证，这是一个非常令人兴奋的人！我预测杰夫在他在亚马逊的所有角色都是他的新角色时，杰夫将是惊人的。轻松预测。杰夫恭喜你。
note_en:
Another Jeff is moving on from his longtime Amazon executive role, filling out the picture of what has become a massive leadership transition at the tech giant.
 Jeff Blackburn, the senior vice president who played a key behind-the-scenes role as one of Jeff Bezos’ top lieutenants for more than two decades, is leaving Amazon as of today, following  a year-long sabbatical, he wrote in a message to Amazon’s senior leaders, posted to the company’s intranet and obtained by GeekWire.
 “I’m not retiring and will have news on what’s next for me soon,” Blackburn wrote.
  The company isn’t saying where Blackburn is expected to land next, but Bezos made it clear that it’s not likely to be a subject of contention.
 Blackburn “has kept me in the loop on his next chapter, and I can assure you it’s a very exciting one!” Bezos wrote in his response, also posted Tuesday to the Amazon intranet. “I predict that Jeff will be every bit as amazing in his new role as he’s been in all his roles at Amazon. Easy prediction. Big congrats to you, Jeff.” [See full messages from Bezos and Blackburn below.]
 Much has changed in Amazon’s executive suite in Blackburn’s year away.
 Bezos  this month announced plans to step down as CEO in the third quarter of this year, shifting to a role as executive chairman, replaced as CEO by Andy Jassy, the longtime Amazon Web Services leader.
 Jeff Wilke, Amazon’s Worldwide Consumer CEO,  announced in October that he would be leaving in the first quarter of this year, replace by Dave Clark, the former head of worldwide operations.
 Blackburn showed his sense of humor in his message to the senior leadership team, or s-team, joking about one challenge that his departure will resolve.
  “The ‘too many Jeff’s’ bug that’s been bothering many of you for two decades has been fixed!” he wrote. “Over the years, we’ve always had multiple Jeff’s on s-team—Jeff Wilke, Jeff B, myself, and for a time Jeff Holden.  It’s actually how I became “jblack” to many of you and to employees across Amazon.  Wilke’s retirement meant one less Jeff, and today, I’m taking it down to just the original.”
 Like Wilke, Blackburn had been viewed previously as a potential successor to Bezos as CEO, not for continuity of first names, but for depth of experience. As Blackburn noted in memo, his association with Amazon started when he was a junior investment banker at Deutsche Bank, assigned to work on the Amazon IPO. During his more than 21 years of active work at the company, Blackburn led key businesses including Amazon’s third-party sales, Prime Video, Amazon Music and Amazon Advertising, working alongside Bezos on key business initiatives.
 In his message Tuesday, Blackburn recalled one pivotal moment: “In early 1998, I was told by the legendary CFO Joy Covey, that the website might become more than books… ‘you need to just come up here now, trust me’. Wow, you mean like Tapes & CDs & VHS? Done.”
  He also navigated public challenges along the way. In 2018, Blackburn faced criticism from Amazon Studios employees for what they reportedly perceived as him glossing over the circumstances of studio chief Roy Price’s resignation following a sexual harassment allegation against Price. Speaking with employees, Blackburn “sought to cast the situation as a fresh start for an organization already undertaking a bold new strategy,” but his message “frustrated some employees,” news publication The Information  reported at the time.
  One effect of Blackburn’s departure will be to give Jassy a chance to reshape more of the Amazon leadership team as he assumes the reins as CEO later this year. A key announcement that has yet to be made by the company is who will succeed Jassy as CEO of AWS, the company’s fast-growing and profitable cloud division.
 In his message to the leadership team, Blackburn said he hopes that he pushed them and Amazon “to be even more bold, see around corners, think big,” during his tenure. “With the core business in as strong a position as it’s ever been, I know that’s what customers will want from you over the next decade.”
 Here is Blackburn’s message, followed by Bezos’ reply, as posted to Amazon’s intranet.
 Steam: I have an important update for you all, one I know you’ve been waiting on for a long time. The “too many Jeff’s” bug that’s been bothering many of you for two decades has been fixed! Over the years, we’ve always had multiple Jeff’s on s-team—Jeff Wilke, Jeff B, myself, and for a time Jeff Holden. It’s actually how I became “jblack” to many of you and to employees across Amazon. Wilke’s retirement meant one less Jeff, and today, I’m taking it down to just the original.
 I am officially leaving Amazon today. In late 1996, I was a junior investment banker at Deutsche Bank, assigned to a potential Amazon IPO project. I did the excel models, wrote chunks of the AMZN IPO prospectus, etc. One of the things that sticks with me to this day is that I was the one who carried all the team’s heavier bags during our three weeks of global travel. There was no Zoom or Chime, so it was quite a grueling process back then.
 In early 1998, I was told by the legendary CFO Joy Covey, that the website might become more than books… “you need to just come up here now, trust me”. Wow, you mean like Tapes & CDs & VHS? Done.
 I joined Amazon full-time soon thereafter. I would do a lot of building with you all – 3P marketplace, M&A and BusDev teams, A9, Advertising, Amazon Music, Prime Video, Amazon Studios. During my 22 years, I’d like to think I never stopped helping carry those heavier bags. The newer initiatives, the ones that took extra courage and long-term thinking by our team. And I hope that, on the margin, I pushed s-team and Amazon to be even more bold, see around corners, think big. With the core business in as strong a position as it’s ever been, I know that’s what customers will want from you over the next decade.
 Terrific new s-team leaders have stepped into these big roles and are continuing to build and boldly invent. I admire and particularly commend your leadership for the past 11 months: watching the company execute during COVID has been astonishing – your teams deliver Amazon’s best when the best is most needed.
 I want to thank you all for your friendship, teamwork, and support. What an amazing 22 years it has been together – I will cherish it, forever. I’m not retiring and will have news on what’s next for me soon.
 Of course, today, I am formally giving up “jblack@”. I have just one request—that I forever stay “jblack” in the halls of Amazon. It’d be an honor,
 It was a lucky day for us when you got assigned to that IPO roadshow. Watching you as that junior investment banker, it was easy to see your wicked smarts — fully on display. But who knew you’d be so driven by a relentless urge to invent and pioneer? From marketplace and advertising to the launch of Prime Video and Amazon Studios, you’ve been a builder. And you’ve helped all of us in everything — not just the things you directly led. You have judgment and your viewpoint is never redundant. When you talk, I find myself leaning in and listening carefully — always.
 Thank you for more than two decades of leadership. For pushing us all to keep exploring. For carrying all of those bags.
 Though it’s not quite ready to be shared, jblack has kept me in the loop on his next chapter, and I can assure you it’s a very exciting one! I predict that Jeff will be every bit as amazing in his new role as he’s been in all his roles at Amazon. Easy prediction. Big congrats to you, Jeff.
title: 内部备忘录：亚马逊SVP Jeff Blackburn，杰夫贝斯近22年来，他曾在一年长期的休假后离开公司
title_en: Internal memo: Amazon SVP Jeff Blackburn, a close lieutenant to Jeff Bezos for more than 22 years, says he is leaving the company, after a year-long sabbatical

note:
最近，消费者互联网-专门用于建立大型人群网络并从中获利的产品集-开始变得相当混乱。在过去的五年中，一个空空荡荡的空间再次充满了生命。这些产品引人注目，并且增长速度足够快，以至于Facebook和其他公司已开始尝试进行反向工程和复制。
 在我看来，它仍然不是很真实，但是到处都可以看到迹象：社交网络再次具有竞争力。
 今天，让我们参观这个奇怪的新局面，并讨论对科技巨头和试图遏制它们的政府意味着什么-并不意味着什么。
  如果我必须确定美国社交网络之间的竞争何时结束，我会选择2016年8月2日。那是Instagram推出其Snapchat故事副本的时候，这削弱了暴发户挑战者的势头并发出了寒意。通过启动生态系统。
 我认为复制功能不一定具有反竞争性-实际上，正如我将在下面讨论的那样，这表明该生态系统正在按预期运行-但是，Facebook在此处复制的效果是巨大的。 Snap陷入了漫长的困境，潜在的企业家和投资者得到了这样的信息：Facebook将寻求收购或复制任何暴发户的社交产品，从而极大地限制了其突破成功的几率。投资相应减少。
 上一年，在Twitter的Periscope应用程序获得成功之后，Facebook复制了其实时视频功能，并且对这两种产品的热情似乎在逐渐减弱。当现场直播视频在Houseparty的帮助下取得了短暂的成功时，Facebook也将其克隆了下来，Houseparty随后以未公开的价格出售给Epic Games。
 在这个停滞的环境中，包括我在内的许多人开始相信，让Facebook收购Instagram和WhatsApp是错误的。前者成为年轻一代的突破性社交网络，后者巩固了Facebook在全球交流中的主导地位。即使两个人都没有像在Facebook统治下那样规模化，但两个人都保持独立的世界将会更具竞争力。 
这是联邦贸易委员会对公司的反托拉斯诉讼的基本论文，它于12月提交。政府认为，通过多年来，Facebook“是非法维护其个人社交网络垄断的跨越竞争行为，”如果成功可以迫使Facebook销售Instagram和Whatsapp。这是一个棘手的案例;正如本汤普森在这里解释的那样，政府试图定义Facebook竞争的市场，以证明它具有垄断的垄断。
 您可以认为FTC对Facebook的案例疲软，并认为2016年至2021年的期间，美国社交网络中的创新非常小，至少在他们激励的基本用户行为方面。社交产品市场变得令人难以置信; Facebook和谷歌在数字广告中建立了二寡头;他们的巨大尺寸和不可预测的效果有助于引发对美国科技巨头的全球反弹。
 如果像我一样，你认为这是一个问题，你可以争论两个基本方法中的一个来解决它。首先是政府干预，以反垄断诉讼或国会的新法规，这将规定科技巨头收购较小公司的能力，或者为进入市场进入市场或竞争的新障碍。第二个是基本上做了什么，信任宇宙的熵性和不可吸入的时间，最终会恢复竞争。
 如果第二选择听起来很荒谬，那不是没有先例。在20世纪90年代末，微软对PC市场的主导地位带领政府对公司的举动来追求反托拉斯案，以将其Internet Explorer浏览器与Windows操作系统捆绑在一起。担心的是，这种捆绑将永远赋予消费者PC市场的微软总权力。实际上，当然，移动电话就在那里只是等待被完善，然后苹果进一步了，现在没有人真正担心微软对PC市场的权力。
 我希望美国政府介入2016年左右，探讨科技巨头合并和收购的新条例。在缺席的情况下，我们只能下注熵 - 无论哪种逆势资本家仍然认为他们在市场上挑战Facebook，尽管有很多优势。
 但是，这是一堆逆势资本家所做的。最近他们一直在成功。
  Facebook在2021年的最大竞争对手当然是Tiktok，它是在2018年在美国在美国推出的Facebook的应用程序中的虹吸使用（与音乐信息合并后）。 
Tiktok通过使人们更容易地使人们更容易，即使您不知道或遵循单个人，也可以令人难以置信的令人信服的视频，并伴随着令人难以置信的引人注目，并且最终创建了一个音频模因的整个宇宙，视觉效果和社区in-jokes。
 Eugene Wei，Tiktok的最佳作家和思想家，在周日晚上在App上发布了他的论文系列的第三部分。在许多突出点中，魏使得进入Tiktok的成功的庞大力量使Facebook（或YouTube）难以克隆。他写：
 人们将instagram复制snapchat＆＃39; s故事特征直到时间结束，但事实是格式是n＆＃39; t＆＃39; t ever将成为一些可靠的护城河。短术是一个聪明的新维度，可以改变社交媒体，但它很容易可复制。
 这就是为什么Tiktok＆＃39;网络效应的创造性物质。克隆Tiktok，您可以＆＃39; t只是复制任何一个功能。它＆＃39;＆所有这些，而不仅仅是特征，而且用户如何部署它们以及所得视频如何在FYP Feed上相互交互。它＆＃39; s复制内置于Tiktok＆＃39; s生态系统中的所有反馈循环，所有这些反馈循环都是互联的。也许你可以复制一些原子，但魔术在分子水平处生活。
 Tiktok的成功是Facebook内部真正焦虑的源泉，员工在几乎每一个全手Q＆amp期间问CEO Mark Zuckerberg一个关于它的问题。该公司已部署了一个竞争对手，称为卷轴，在Instagram内部，也许会找到成功的方法。但是，更大的一点是，无论赔率如何，Facebook现在必须与Tiktok竞争，或者风险失去下一代。
 但是，你可能已经被认为是。 （除非你是FTC，除了在整个对Facebook所谓的垄断位置的诉讼中显着避免了Tiktok的任何提及。）但是当涉及移动短窗体视频时，Facebook和YouTube面临着真正的挑战。
  对于初学者，有音频。虽然仍然可以通过邀请，Clubhouse最近达到估计1000万下载。包括Tiffany Haddish，Elon Musk，Joe Rogan和Zuckerberg在内的名人在这个应用程序上表现出色，授予它在社交创业中罕见的文化音乐罕见，仍然不到一年。 Clubhouse上个月筹集了10亿美元的估价 - 超过Facebook的支付最终为Instagram支付。 
因为它是一个音频应用程序，Clubhouse并没有提出Tiktok所做的相当威胁：您仍然可以在听俱乐部聊天时理论上浏览Instagram或消息业务。但是，根据纽约时报的报告，Facebook已经充分地兴起了它现在正在努力弥补如何克隆该应用程序的应用程序。在其他地方，Twitter已经有一个俱乐部克隆，称为空间，在测试版中。目前尚不清楚，俱乐部房间对任一公司构成威胁。但两者仍然将其视为挑战。
  据报道，经过多年的技术挑战技术挑战媒体，涉及视频，增强现实和虚拟现实，据报道，Facebook举行了第二次来看文本。过去一年中的重塑的崛起已经开始造成百万富翁，基于文本的创造者的兴起，同时也将数百万人从社会饲料中拉到电子邮件收件箱的相对平静之中。 （当然，我有一个个人股份;我在很大程度上开始了一份通讯，因为我的社交饲料感觉像一个糟糕的地方来获得新闻。）
 这里有趣的是，Facebook现在似乎也对这种可能性开放了。上个月的时候，次数还报告了Facebook正在为记者和作家开发新闻稿工具。 （我已经用自己的来源证实了这一点。）与Clubhouse一样，新闻通讯几乎没有对Facebook构成存在的威胁。但他们可以从公司的应用程序中出现流血和注意力 - 在一些国家在Facebook上的新闻可能无法提供的世界，它可能是一个对冲。 （并且Twitter也清楚地思考：它是上个月收购了Superach竞争对手的报道。）
 这使Facebook竞争了跨音频，视频和文本的合法快速增长的竞争对手。虽然它在早期的阶段，但我认为公司也可能在摄影中有一个有趣的竞争对手。
 DISPO是一个邀请的社交照片应用程序，具有扭曲：您无法在您拍摄时24小时内看到您的照片。 （该应用程序向您发送一个推送通知，每天在当地时间上午9点打开它们：除其他内容中，一个漂亮的黑客攻击每日使用。一年的基本效用。但上个月，具有社交功能的Beta版本，包括在iOS上推出的共享照片“卷”，并在Apple的Testflight软件上迅速达到10,000人帽。它在10月份筹集了400万美元的种子资金，并假设嗡嗡声继续进入公开发布，如果以一种主要的方式起飞，我不会感到惊讶。
 音频，视频，照片和文本：在某种程度上，Facebook从未停止过竞争这些尺寸在公司的历史中。但我不记得上次争取这么多有趣的战斗的最后一次。
  这就是我争论社交网络再次竞争的时候我不说的是什么。 
Facebook不再受到反托拉斯审查，或者美国政府（以及分别的美国律师将军）应该放弃诉讼。
 考虑到所有这一新的竞争，应该允许Facebook在未来购买竞争对手社会网络。
 很长一段时间来说，Facebook不会留下世界上最大的社交网络，或者其业务将在短期内遭受。
 事实上，我认为有一个很好的案例，尤其是来自美国政府的反托拉斯压力是允许竞争首先返回社交网络。在2013年或2014年出现了俱乐部或俱乐部，并不难以想象Facebook Racing来获取它们并将它们从棋盘上击倒。但在2021年，当Facebook面临英国面临正式的反垄断审查时，通过收购失败的GIF搜索引擎，公司只能坐下来试着复制别人做得更好。
 如果是这样的话，它表明，在过去半年过去十年里，对Facebook的巨大统治的半成年反应让我们姗姗来迟，更好的地方。抗托拉斯压力使公司赚取收购极为困难，开设一个足够大的窗户，以便新进入者攀登。它仍有待观察到Facebook，YouTube或Twitter有多大的挑战可以增长。但是在很长一段时间内第一次，我对他们的机会持乐观态度。
    ⬆️趋势：Facebook在该国政变后删除了缅甸军队的主页。给出Facebook在早期的军用背面种族灭绝的角色，动作在权衡潜在风险时恰当。 （路透社）
  🔃横向趋势：Facebook的政治广告禁令无意中阻止了购买Pro-accination广告的人。至少110个组有Pro-accine广告被拒绝，一个标志，基于AI的系统无效的迹象仍然存在。 （Darius Tahir / Politico） 
Trend️趋势下降：微软与欧盟的发行商共同做出了愤世嫉俗的努力，将澳大利亚式的重组引入了欧洲的Facebook和Google。正如加布·里维拉（Gabe Rivera）所言，此举将“破坏基本的互联网自由链接，进一步向较小的发行商倾斜竞争环境，扼杀婴儿床中未来的初创商业模式，并使鲁珀特·默多克（Rupert Murdoch）更强大，所有这些给Google带来了一些不便。 ” （陈凯文/美联社）
   ⭐学者对近期工作的评论发现，人们对社会网络与民主并存感到越来越悲观。在与研究人员阅读和发送电子邮件时，Thomas B. Edsall发现了令人不安的图画。他在《纽约时报》上对此进行了撰写，其广泛的观点值得一读：
 当被问及远景前景时，佩里西说，没有确切的答案。他担心“对民主进程缺乏信任，这种信任在过去四年中恶化并在1月6日爆发，将产生严重而持久的影响。”
 ⭐一个关于由假名前雇员建立ByteDance审查制度的有趣文章。 “事实是，政治演讲只包含一小部分已删除的内容。中国网民精通自我检查，不知所措。 ……但是政治言论仍然笼罩在大块。” （ 协议）
 州一级的隐私法正在激增，甚至威胁到在美国范围内互联网的分裂。弗吉尼亚州，纽约州，明尼苏达州，俄克拉荷马州和佛罗里达州都在考虑制定新的隐私法。国家隐私法的进展仍然遥遥无期。 （戴维·乌贝蒂（David Uberti）/《华尔街日报》
 谷歌最终将在周三取消对美国政治广告的禁令。我认为Google掌握了这一时机……基本上对吗？ （Sara Fischer / Axios）
 ⭐看一下Facebook内部越来越大的内部压力（以及适当的内部压力），以将其内容政策团队与其游说机构分开。前Facebook安全负责人Alex Stamos在此表示：“您不希望平台政策制定者向负责使政府满意的人汇报工作。” “乔尔[卡普兰]来自布什白宫，政府关系在言论要求上没有中立立场。” （Ryan Mac和Craig Silverman / BuzzFeed） 
纽约金融服务部批评Facebook没有采取更多措施来防止收集有关人们医疗状况的未授权数据。此前有报道称，包括流行时期和怀孕追踪器Flo在内的健康应用程序正在与Facebook共享数据。（Jeff Horwitz /《华尔街日报》）
 Parler的创始人曾被Parler短暂禁止。但是在BuzzFeed的询问后，禁令被撤销。 （Ryan Mac / BuzzFeed）
 “横向阅读”是一种通过阅读多种资源来学习某个主题的实践，它可能是帮助人们避免阴谋充斥的兔子洞的关键。温哥华华盛顿州立大学的媒体专家迈克尔·考菲尔德（Michael Caufield）在一次采访中阐述了他对数字素养的“ SIFT”方法。 （查理·沃泽尔/纽约时报）
 这篇文章认为，牛津互联网学院最近的一份报告称虚假信息领域的快速增长存在缺陷。近年来，新闻界对这一问题的高度关注反映出对该主题的兴趣日益增长，但未必影响机构的工作便爆炸式增长。 （Emma Briant /有线）
 欧洲联盟与联合王国达成了一项数据共享协议。企业松了一口气。 （Aoife White和Natalia Drozdiak / Bloomberg）
 一家马来西亚新闻媒体因在其中一篇文章上发表评论而被判of视法庭罪。没有第230节的保护，生活就是这样。 （上帝禁止这样做，这是“有损公众对司法机构的信任”。）（纳利尼·埃卢马莱（Nalini Elumalai）/第19条）

关于'CULT'而不是社区的俱乐部？ “舞台”和宿主批准的客人的型号为邪教创造了一个普遍的层次结构，这部分来自前者的Facebook执行官。 （SAM LISHIN /信息）  Twitter探索了买印度社会公司Sharechat。 除了其他事情之外，ShareChat Make Moj，自Tiktok在该国禁止以来繁殖的短型视频应用程序。 （曼鱼辛格/ TechCrunch）  Koo如何成为印度的印度民族主义批准的Twitter替代品。 KOO是一个为一个拥有超过700个方言的国家的本地语言扬声器而建立的，刚刚跨过300万用户。 （Nilesh Christopher /世界其他地区）  发送给我提示，评论，问题以及平台竞争对手的列表：casey@platformer.news。
note_en:
Lately, the consumer internet — that set of products devoted to building and monetizing large networks of people — has started to feel rather buzzy. A space that had been largely emptied out over the past five years is once again humming with life. The products are compelling enough, and growing fast enough, that Facebook and others have begun trying to reverse-engineer and copy them.
 It still doesn’t seem quite real to me, and yet everywhere I look the signs are there: social networks are competitive again.
 Today, let’s tour this weird new landscape, and talk about what it means — and doesn’t mean — for the tech giants and the governments trying to rein them in.
  If I had to put a date on when competition ended among social networks in the United States, I’d choose Aug. 2, 2016. That’s when Instagram  introduced its copy of Snapchat stories, blunting the momentum of an upstart challenger and sending a chill through the startup ecosystem.
 I don’t think copying features is necessarily anti-competitive — in fact, as I’ll argue below, it’s a sign that the ecosystem is working as intended — but the effect of Facebook’s copying here was dramatic. Snap fell into a long funk, and would-be entrepreneurs and investors got the message: Facebook will seek to acquire or copy any upstart social product, dramatically limiting its odds of breakout success. Investment shrunk accordingly.
 The previous year, after the success of Twitter’s Periscope app, Facebook had cloned its live video features, and enthusiasm for both products seemed to broadly peter out. When live group video experienced momentary success under Houseparty,  Facebook cloned that too, and Houseparty later sold to Epic Games for an undisclosed sum.
 It was in this stagnant environment that many people, myself included, came to believe that it had been a mistake to let Facebook acquire Instagram and WhatsApp. The former became the breakout social network of a younger generation, and the latter cemented Facebook’s global dominance in communication. A world in which both had remained independent would have been much more competitive, even if neither had grown to the scale that they did under Facebook.
 This is the basic thesis of  the Federal Trade Commission’s antitrust lawsuit against the company, which it filed in December. The government argues that Facebook “is illegally maintaining its personal social networking monopoly through a years-long course of anticompetitive conduct,” and if successful could force Facebook to sell off Instagram and WhatsApp. It’s a tricky case;  as Ben Thompson explains here, the government’s attempt to define the market in which Facebook competes so as to prove it has a monopoly is rather tortured.
 You can think the FTC’s case against Facebook is weak and also believe that the period from 2016 to 2021 saw remarkably little innovation among American social networks, at least in terms of the basic user behaviors that they inspire. The market for social products became incredibly concentrated; Facebook and Google built a duopoly in digital advertising; and their vast size and unpredictable effects helped to trigger a global backlash against American tech giants.
 If, like me, you think this is all a problem, you could argue for one of two basic approaches to fixing it. The first is government intervention, in the form of an antitrust lawsuit or new regulations from Congress, that would regulate the ability of tech giants to acquire smaller companies or put up new barriers to entering the market or competing on fair terms. The second is to do basically nothing, trusting that the entropic nature of the universe and the inexorable march of time would eventually restore competition.
 If the second choice sounds ridiculous, it is not without precedent. In the late 1990s, Microsoft’s dominance over the PC market led the government to pursue an antitrust case over the company’s move to bundle its Internet Explorer browser with the Windows operating system. The fear was that such bundling would grant Microsoft total power over the consumer PC market forever. In reality, of course, mobile phones were out there just waiting to be perfected, and then Apple came along and did just that, and now no one really worries too much about Microsoft’s power over the PC market.
 I do wish the US government had intervened around 2016 to explore new regulations for tech giants’ mergers and acquisitions. In its absence, we could only bet on entropy — and whichever contrarian capitalists still felt like they could challenge Facebook in the market despite its many advantages.
 The thing is, though, that a bunch of contrarian capitalists did. And lately they have been having a lot of success.
  Facebook’s biggest competitor in 2021 is, of course, TikTok, which has been siphoning usage from Facebook’s family of apps since it launched in the United States in 2018 (after merging with Musical.ly).
 TikTok began by making it dramatically easier for people to make compelling videos, parceled out fame and fortune with a central feed that is incredibly compelling even if you don’t know or follow a single person, and eventually created an entire universe of audio memes, visual effects, and community in-jokes.
 Eugene Wei, our best  writer and  thinker on TikTok, published the third part of his essay series on the app Sunday night. Among the many salient points Wei makes is that the sheer number of forces that have gone into TikTok’s success have made it difficult for Facebook (or  YouTube) to clone.  He writes:
 People will litigate Instagram copying Snapchat's Stories feature until the end of time, but the fact is that format wasn't ever going to be some defensible moat. Ephemerality is a clever new dimension on which to vary social media, but it's easily copiable.
 This is why TikTok's network effects of creativity matter. To clone TikTok, you can't just copy any single feature. It's all of that, and not just the features, but how users deploy them and how the resultant videos interact with each other on the FYP feed. It's replicating all the feedback loops that are built into TikTok's ecosystem, all of which are interconnected. Maybe you can copy some of the atoms, but the magic lives at the molecular level.
 The success of TikTok is a source of real anxiety inside Facebook, where employees ask CEO Mark Zuckerberg a question about it during nearly every all-hands Q&A session. The company has deployed a competitor, called Reels, inside of Instagram, and perhaps it will find a way to succeed. But the larger point is that, whatever the odds, Facebook now has to compete against TiKTok, or risk losing the next generation.
 You’ve probably already considered that, though. (Unless you’re the FTC, which conspicuously avoided any mention on TikTok in its entire complaint about Facebook’s alleged monopoly position.) But when it comes to mobile short-form video, Facebook and YouTube face a real challenge.
  For starters, there’s audio. While still available only by invitation, Clubhouse  recently hit an estimated 10 million downloads. Celebrities including Tiffany Haddish, Elon Musk, Joe Rogan, and Zuckerberg himself have made appearances on the app, granting it a cultural cachet rare in a social startup that is still less than a year old. Clubhouse raised money last month at a valuation of $1 billion — more than Facebook paid ultimately paid for Instagram.
 Because it’s an audio app, Clubhouse doesn’t pose quite the existential threat that TikTok does: you can still theoretically browse Instagram or message businesses on WhatsApp while listening to a Clubhouse chat. But Facebook has been sufficiently intrigued by Clubhouse’s rapid rise that it is now working out how to clone the app,  according to a report this month in the   New York Times. Elsewhere, Twitter already has a Clubhouse clone,  called Spaces, in beta. It’s not clear that Clubhouse poses a threat to either company, exactly. But both are still taking it as a challenge.
  After years of making its most prominent investments in technically challenging media involving video, augmented reality, and virtual reality, Facebook is reportedly taking a second look at text. The rise of Substack over the past year has begun to mint a growing number of millionaire, text-based creators, while also pulling millions of people away from their social feeds into the relative calm of the email inbox. (I have a personal stake in this one, of course; I started a newsletter in large part because my social feeds had come to feel like a lousy place to get my news.)
 What’s interesting here is that Facebook now seems open to this possibility, too. Last month the  Times also reported that Facebook is developing newsletter tools for reporters and writers. (I’ve confirmed this with my own sources.) As with Clubhouse, newsletters hardly pose an existential threat to Facebook. But they do bleed time and attention away from the company’s apps — and in a world where news may not be even available on Facebook in some countries, it may be wise for it to have a hedge. (And Twitter clearly thinks so, too:  it acquired Substack competitor Revue last month.)
 That leaves Facebook competing with legitimately fast-growing, well funded competitors across audio, video, and text. And while it’s in a much earlier stage, I think the company may soon have an interesting competitor in photography as well.
 Dispo is an invite-only social photo app with a twist: you can’t see any photos you take with the app until 24 hours after you take them. (The app sends you a push notification to open them every day at 9AM local time: among other things, a nice hack to boost daily usage.) Founded by David Dobrik, one of the world’s most popular YouTubers, Dispo has been around as  a basic utility for a year. But last month a beta version with social features including shared photo “rolls” launched on iOS, and quickly hit the 10,000-person cap on Apple’s TestFlight software. It  raised $4 million in seed funding in October, and assuming the buzz continues into a public launch, I wouldn’t be surprised if Dispo took off in a major way.
 Audio, video, photos, and text: to some extent, Facebook has never had to stop competing across these dimensions in the company’s history. But I can’t remember the last time it was fighting so many interesting battles  at the same time.
  Here’s what I’m  not saying when I argue that social networks are competitive again.
 That Facebook should no longer be subject to antitrust scrutiny, or that the US government (and, separately, a coalition of US attorneys general) should abandon their lawsuits.
 That, given all this new competition, Facebook should be allowed to purchase rival social networks in the future.
 That Facebook won’t remain the world’s largest social network for a long time to come, or that its business will suffer in the short term.
 In fact, I think there’s a good case to be made that antitrust pressure from the US government in particular is what has allowed competition to return to social networks in the first place. Had Clubhouse or Substack emerged in 2013 or 2014, it’s not hard to imagine Facebook racing to acquire them and knock them off the chessboard. But in 2021, when Facebook  faces a formal antitrust review in the United Kingdom over its acquisition of a failing GIF search engine, the company can only sit back and try to copy what others are doing better.
 If that’s the case, it suggests that the half-assed response to Facebook’s growing dominance over the past half-decade nonetheless got us, however belatedly, to a better place. Antitrust pressure made it extremely difficult for the company to make acquisitions, opening a window just big enough for new entrants to climb through. It remains to be seen how big any new challengers to Facebook, YouTube, or Twitter can grow. But for the first time in a long time, I’m optimistic about their chances.
    ⬆️  Trending up:   Facebook  removed the main page of the Myanmar military after a coup in the country. Given Facebook’s role in an earlier military-backed genocide there, the move feels appropriate when weighed against the potential risks. (Reuters)
  🔃  Trending sideways:   Facebook ’s political ad ban has inadvertently prevented people from buying pro-vaccination ads. At least 110 groups had pro-vaccine ads rejected, a sign of how ineffective AI-based systems remain for this sort of thing. (Darius Tahir /  Politico)
 ⬇️  Trending down:   Microsoft  joined a cynical effort with publishers in the European Union to bring Australia-style shakedowns to   Facebook  and   Google  in Europe. As Gabe Rivera  put it, the move would “destroy the basic internet freedom to link, further tilt the playing field against smaller publishers, smother future startup business models in the crib, and make  Rupert Murdoch more powerful, all to inconvenience Google a bit.” (Kelvin Chan / AP)
   ⭐  A review of recent work from academics finds growing pessimism about the coexistence of social networks and democracy. In reading and emailing with researchers, Thomas B. Edsall finds a disturbing picture. He writes about it in the  New York Times, and the wide range of perspectives is worth reading:
 Asked what the long-range prospects are, Persily said there was no definitive answer. He worries “that the lack of trust in the democratic process, that festered over the last four years and exploded on Jan. 6, will have a severe and long-lasting impact.”
 ⭐  A fascinating account about building ByteDance’s censorship systems from a pseudonymous former employee. “The truth is, political speech comprised a tiny fraction of deleted content. Chinese netizens are fluent in self-censorship and know what not to say. … But political speech still looms large.” ( Protocol)
 State-level privacy laws are proliferating, threatening to splinter the internet even within the United States. Virginia, New York, Minnesota, Oklahoma and Florida are all considering new privacy laws; progress on a national privacy law remains elusive. (David Uberti /  Wall Street Journal)
 Google  will finally lift its ban on political ads in the United States on Wednesday. I think Google got the timing on this one … basically right? (Sara Fischer /  Axios)
 ⭐  A look at the growing (and appropriate) internal pressure inside   Facebook  to separate its content policy team from its lobbying apparatus. “You don’t want platform policy people reporting to someone who’s in charge of keeping people in government happy,” ex-Facebook security chief  Alex Stamos says here. “ Joel [Kaplan] comes from the Bush White House, and government relations does not have a neutral position on speech requests.” (Ryan Mac and Craig Silverman /  BuzzFeed)
 The New York Department of Financial Services criticized   Facebook  for not doing more to prevent the collection of unauthorized data about people’s medical conditions. The move followed a report that health apps including the popular period and pregnancy tracker  Flo were sharing data with Facebook.(Jeff Horwitz /  Wall Street Journal)
 Parler ’s founder was briefly banned by   Parler. But the ban was reversed after an inquiry from  BuzzFeed. (Ryan Mac /  BuzzFeed)
 “Lateral reading” — the practice of learning about a subject by reading multiple sources — can be a key to helping people avoiding conspiracy-laden rabbit holes. Michael Caufield, a media expert at Washington State University Vancouver, lays out his “SIFT” approach to digital literacy in an interview. (Charlie Warzel /  New York Times)
 A recent Oxford Internet Institute report alleging rapid growth in the field of disinformation was flawed, this piece argues. Heightened press attention to the problem in recent years reflects growing interest in the subject but not necessarily an explosion in influence operations. (Emma Briant /  Wired)
 The European Union reached a data-sharing agreement with the United Kingdom. Businesses are breathing a sigh of relief. (Aoife White and Natalia Drozdiak / Bloomberg)
 A Malaysian news outlet was found guilty of contempt of court over a comment posted on one of its articles. This is what life can look like without Section 230 protections. (The comment was found to “threaten public confidence in the judiciary,” God forbid.) (Nalini Elumalai /  Article 19)
   ⭐   Margaret Mitchell , the co-lead of   Google’s  ethical AI team, said she was fired after an investigation into her use of corporate email. Mitchell had reportedly used automated scripts to search her corporate email to find evidence of discrimination against her former co-lead,  Timnit Gebru. Ina Fried has the story at  Axios:
 Google was already under fire for its ouster of Timnit Gebru, the other co-lead of the team. Mitchell has been locked out of the corporate email since last month after what a source says was her effort to search corporate correspondence for evidence to back up Gebru's claim of discrimination and harassment.
 The move comes the same day that Google  announced internally it had completed its investigation of Gebru's exit, as Axios first reported. The company didn't release the findings of that investigation, but announced a series of policy changes, including tying executive pay companywide to diversity and inclusion efforts.
 WhatsApp  will retain limited functionality for users who do not accept its new privacy policy by May 15. The lighter-touch approach may assuage concerns from users who flocked to  Signal and  Telegram after changes were announced last month. (Manish Singh /  TechCrunch)
 Alphabet  is in talks to bring   Google  News back to Spain. The company shut the service down in 2014 when the country passed a law that would have forced it to pay a licensing fee for linking to headlines and article snippets. (Emma Pinedo / Reuters)
 Spotify  is developing a high-fidelity tier to launch later this year. Hi-fi options have long been available on  Tidal, and more recently,  Amazon Music. (Chris Welch /  The Verge)
  LinkedIn  is developing a marketplace for freelancers. With 740 million users, it would appear to be poised for success. (Amir Efrati /  The Information)
 YouTube  removed a   PewDiePie  video that attacked popular kids’ channel   Cocomelon. It’s a sign that the platform is taking creator-on-creator harassment more seriously. (Lucas Shaw / Bloomberg)
 Is   Clubhouse  about ‘cult’ rather than community? The model of a “stage” and host-approved guests creates a hierarchy common to cults, this piece from a former  Facebook executive argues. (Sam Lessin /  The Information)
 Twitter  explored buying   Indian  social company   ShareChat. Among other things, ShareChat makes  Moj, a short-form video app that has thrived since  TikTok was banned in the country. (Manish Singh /  TechCrunch)
 How   Koo  became India’s Hindu nationalist–approved   Twitter  alternative. Koo, which is built for local-language speakers in a country with more than 700 dialects, just crossed 3 million users. (Nilesh Christopher /  Rest of World)

客户数据分析公司BlueShift今天宣布已关闭3000万美元C系列，使其总额筹集到6500万美元。该公司计划将资金放在扩大其劳动力，同时进一步开发其营销平台。
 Forrester Research最近的一份报告发现，高度个性化的Omnichannel营销活动有可能产生4倍的收入和比静态运动更大的收入增长18倍。另一个调查挂钩Umnichannel的平均参与率为19％，而单通道努力的5.4％则为5.4％。
  技术支持Blueshift的营销平台，称为互动图，从零售和电子商务网站，数字媒体品牌，消费者金融公司和旅游业务商店的产品和内容互动。它不断增强了新的属性，如信道接合和亲和力，它们存储在单个索引中。
  互动图使营销经理能够在一个地方管理电子邮件，推送通知，短信，社交媒体帐户和网页。他们可以通过蓝色的个性化工作室仪表板进行测试和优化内容，允许它们按行为和人口划分用户。专有的算法表格预测分数，以确定哪些用户具有完成各种操作的高或低可能性。互动图计算基于事务数据的不同产品线，类别，品牌，作者，艺术家和价格点的用户亲和力。它还突出显示了主动和失效的用户，并且可选地通过对各种消息类型和Web流量的响应来筛选用户。
 BlueShift的套房还提供了基于浏览或购买类似物品的客户的行为的工艺建议的协同过滤。该平台通过应用程序和网站服务，其目标工具允许管理人员在用户的细分中获取，包括放弃物品和购物车的那些，其中包括在ShopIfy的开源液体语言上建立的定制模板。
 去年，BlueShift推出了频道参与评分，这预测用户在诸如电子邮件，推送，应用程序，短信或第三方集成之类的频道上与频道中的消息接触的可能性。在同一时间，该公司首次借待了本土应用程序内的消息，允许营销人员提供信息，以通过船坞，应用更新等来指导用户。
 Cofounder和Ceo Vijay Chittoor表示，Blueshift，其客户包括Discovery，Lendingtree，Udacity和BBC，在Forrester进行的一项研究中提供了781％的投资回报。 “随着数字转型的紧迫性增加，我们对一个[平台]的需求增加了不仅可以统一淤泥数据，而且还统一友好体验，”休斯在新闻稿中说。 
总部位于旧金山的Blueshift最新一次加薪是由Fort Ross Ventures和Avatar Growth Capital领投的。 该轮融资还吸引了包括软银亚洲风险投资公司，风暴风险投资公司，导电风险投资公司和Nexus Venture Partners等现有投资者的参与。  VentureBeat的使命是成为技术决策者的数字城镇广场，以获取有关变革性技术和交易的知识。  我们的网站提供有关数据技术和策略的重要信息，以指导您领导组织。 我们邀请您成为我们社区的成员，可以访问：  成为会员
note_en:
Customer data analytics company  Blueshift today announced it has closed a $30 million series C round, bringing its  total raised to $65 million. The company plans to put the funds toward expanding its workforce while further developing its marketing platform.
 A recent report from Forrester Research found that highly personalized, omnichannel marketing campaigns have the potential to generate 4 times more revenue and 18 times greater profits than static campaigns. Another  survey pegs the average engagement rate for omnichannel at 19% compared with 5.4% for single-channel efforts.
  The tech underpinning Blueshift’s marketing platform, called Interaction Graph, stores product and content interactions from customers of retail and ecommerce sites, digital media brands, consumer finance firms, and travel businesses. It’s continually enhanced with new attributes, like channel engagement and affinity, which are stored in a single index.
  Interaction Graph enables marketing managers to manage emails, push notifications, text messages, social media accounts, and webpages in one place. They can A/B test and optimize content through Blueshift’s Personalization Studio dashboard, which allows them to segment users by behavior and demographic. Proprietary algorithms tabulate predictive scores to identify which users have a high or low possibility of completing various actions. Interaction Graph computes those users’ affinity for different product lines, categories, brands, authors, artists, and price points based on transactional data. It also highlights active and lapsing users and optionally filters users by their responsiveness to various message types and web traffic.
 Blueshift’s suite also features collaborative filtering that crafts recommendations based on the behavior of customers who browsed or purchased similar items. The platform serves these through apps and websites, and its targeting tools allow managers to home in on segments of users, including those who abandon items and carts, with customized templates built on Shopify’s open source Liquid language.
 Last year, Blueshift launched Channel Engagement Scores, which predicts the likelihood of a user engaging with a message on a channel such as email, push, in-app, SMS, or third-party integrations. Around the same time, the company debuted native in-app messages, which allow marketers to provide information to guide users through onboarding, app updates, and more.
 Cofounder and CEO Vijay Chittoor said Blueshift, whose customers include Discovery, LendingTree, Udacity, and the BBC, delivered a 781% return on investment in a study conducted by Forrester. “With the increased urgency toward digital transformation, we have seen an increased demand for a [platform] that can not only unify siloed data, but also unify siloed experiences,” Chittoor said in a press release.
 San Francisco-based Blueshift’s latest raise was led by Fort Ross Ventures and Avatar Growth Capital. The round also saw participation from existing investors, including Softbank Ventures Asia, Storm Ventures, Conductive Ventures, and Nexus Venture Partners.

 在2020年的总统选举前几个月，Tiktok宣布，它将与事实上检查组织配对，作为打击选举和Covid-19错误信息的更广泛努力的一部分。在宣布时，Tiktok受到特朗普政府和立法者的迫在眉睫的压力，据称与中国政府的联系。微软和甲骨文是一家中国企业的百日贸易展览会的一些公司招标。 Oracle和Tiktok之间的最终协议仍然存在。

 在删除选举规则的视频之外，Tiktok周三宣布，从平台的推荐算法或为您的页面中删除了超过441,000个视频，以扩散错误信息。在2020年选举之前不久，Tiktok推出了由Ballotready的选举指南，这是一个投票信息工具。该指南可在“发现”页面和与选举相关的视频，HASHTAG和政治账户中找到。它被访问了18,000,000次。
 Tiktok还在2020年美国大选期间删除了1,750,000次“为自动化”的账户。 “虽然未知的虽然专门用于放大选举相关内容的任何帐户，但要删除这套帐户，以保护平台在这个关键时刻，”透明度报告读取。
note_en:
TikTok removed over 340,000 videos in the US for breaking the platform’s rules on election misinformation, manipulation, or disinformation, according to a Wednesday  transparency report covering content from the second half of last year.
 A few months before the 2020 presidential election, TikTok announced that it would pair with fact-checking organizations as part of a broader effort to combat election and COVID-19 misinformation. At the time of the announcement, TikTok was under immense pressure by the Trump administration and lawmakers for its alleged ties to the Chinese government. Microsoft and Oracle were among some of the companies bidding for ownership over TikTok from ByteDance, a Chinese firm. A final deal  between Oracle and TikTok is still on hold.
  “TikTok is a diverse, global community fueled by creative expression. We work to maintain an environment where everyone feels safe to create, find community, and be entertained,” Michael Beckerman, TikTok’s vice president and head of US public policy, said  in a statement Wednesday. “We are committed to being transparent about how we keep our platform safe, because it helps build trust and understanding with our community.”
 Outside of the videos deleted for breaking election rules, TikTok announced Wednesday that over 441,000 videos were removed from the platform’s recommendation algorithm, or the For You Page, for spreading misinformation. Shortly before the 2020 election, TikTok rolled out an election guide  powered by BallotReady, a voting information tool. That guide was available on the Discover page, and election-related videos, hashtags, and political accounts. It was visited 18,000,000 times.
 TikTok also removed 1,750,000 accounts used “for automation” throughout the course of the 2020 US election. “While it’s not known if any of the accounts were used specifically to amplify election related content, it was important to remove this set of accounts to protect the platform at this critical time,” the transparency report reads.
title: Tiktok表示，它在美国删除了300k +视频在H2 2020中的选举错误信息，以及441K视频，从其推荐页面进行错误信息

Photo: Craig Federighi, Apple's senior vice president of software engineering, unveils Apple's sign-in button in 2019. Photo by AP.
    More and more iPhone apps are offering people the option of signing up by using information stored with Apple, adding to the array of quick sign-in formats that let people skip typing in their email address and other details. But how Apple is implementing this option—requiring it on all apps that offer sign-in options from other firms like Google or Facebook—has upset some developers. And that has prompted complaints that the U.S. Department of Justice is now investigating, said two people with direct knowledge of the situation.
 Apple says its “Sign in with Apple” button, which gives users the ability to hide their personal details from the app, is “pro privacy.” But investigators are examining how Apple uses the sign-in button and other App Store rules to make it more difficult for users to switch to a rival device maker, according to the two people, who have met regularly with the Justice Department over the past 18 months. The government’s interest in the Apple sign-in button, which the company introduced in 2019, hasn’t been previously reported.
    The metaphors we use are like a map. They can guide us or lead us astray. When British mathematician Clive Humby said “Data is the new oil” back in 2006, he meant that data, like oil, must be refined and transformed to make it into useful products and services. Only then does it become valuable. Unfortunately, to too many people the phrase seems to suggest that our personal data is...

    自2015年成立以来，亚马逊的Alexa虚拟助手一直是一种被困在一盒电子产品中的数字形式。原始回声是圆筒，后来的型号具有球形，回声显示装置有效地楔入前面的屏幕。 Alexa在所有这些中都以相同的方式表现：你要求它做某事，然后它提供了礼貌的响应，并做了这件事。
 新的249.99美元的Echo Show 10，它于2月25日开始运输，是第一个不仅仅是扬声器或坐在架子上的扬声器或屏幕。它简单地说，一个移动的alexa设备 - 只是一点点。
 回声显示10的电动基座允许Alexa旋转屏幕，所以它面向您，内置相机和跟踪算法允许它在您使用的情况下保持屏幕朝向您的方向指向。它有点闪耀到未来的Alexa机器人，这可能变得远远超过一个语音动力遥控器。
 然而，它只是一瞥，而新的Echo Show 10中的Alexa在很大程度上是您在先前的回声显示和其他Alexa供电的智能显示器上相同的Alexa。您可以为信息询问 - 天气预报，最喜欢的播放列表，新闻，日历约会，以及它将为您提供礼貌响应，并在屏幕上显示所请求的信息。
   与先前的回声展示模型不同，该模型全部共享类似的楔形形状，回声显示10具有完全新的设计，具有圆柱形基部上方的浮动触摸屏。这允许显示10做前一个版本不能做两件事：倾斜其屏幕并旋转近360度。
 倾斜屏幕要求您手动向前或向后移动屏幕，但旋转会自动发生，这归功于显示底座内的静音无刷电机10.这两个能力使得恰好恰好正确定位屏幕，无论您是正确的吗？正在使用它来在Netflix上观看Bridgerton（是的，Echo Show现在支持Netflix！）或将视频通话放到全国各地的家庭成员。 
随着该电机，底座屋顶屋：3英寸向下朝下的低音扬声器和两个1英寸高音扬声器，位于屏幕下方。这可确保在屏幕面向您时，扬声器也是如此。
 显示器与先前模型相同10.1英寸，1280 x 800触摸屏。它肯定远离您家中的最高分辨率屏幕，但它充满活力，亮起足以从几英尺远看。围绕展示是一个笨重的挡板，这不是一个问题，我会关心，直到亚马逊出来的一个模型，一个有更薄的模型，即立即让这个看起来很傻。
  屏幕的顶部边缘具有静音按钮，音量上下，以及可以使用的快门来阻止相机的视图。现有的10英寸回声显示没有物理快门，尽管亚马逊确实将其添加到较小的回声显示5和回声展示8。
 挡板的右上角是一个新的1300万像素相机。相机是回声显示10的追踪位置并相应地移动自己的能力。亚马逊告诉我它使用Echolocation和Computer Vision来放置您的位置并旋转屏幕，即使在整个房间里移动。所有此处理都在设备上本地发生 - 与您对Alexa的语音命令不同，它们主要在Amazon的服务器上远程处理。您可以在设置中或使用语音命令关闭此移动或简单地幻灯片关闭。

 这些要求使回声显示在您的家中比以前更明显的设备。它必须坐在开放状态下，真正受益于其独特的能力，其微型复古iMac看起来比旧模型更多的关注。我在家里的节目10中找到的最佳位置是在站立的高度台面上，将厨房与用餐室分开。从那里，秀10可以相对自由地旋转，然后在我从厨房移动到餐厅和背部的时候跟进我。
  回声显示只会在您与之交流时移动，因此不要指望它是一个有个性坐在柜台上的人格的小机器人。这是一个令人虐待jibo的最大借鉴，它专门针对其机器人个性而设计，并将“醒来”，转向你，每当你进入房间时都会迎接你。回声显示10将保持静止和休眠，直到您为某些东西询问或触摸屏幕。 
一旦您发出语音命令或点击屏幕，展示10将栩栩如生，旋转本身，使其始终面对您。如果它正在播放音乐，视频，食谱或任何其他持续的事情，在您在房间周围移动时会继续自动旋转。亚马逊吹捧它在遵循配方时特别有用，因为当您在收集成分和以下步骤中移动时，屏幕始终可见。
 对于视频呼叫的视频呼叫来说，它也比先前的回声节目更容易，这总是有一个固定的视野。现在，该节目将使用其更高的分辨率相机进行数字缩放并在视频通话（就像Facebook门户网站上）以保持框架时追踪。添加屏幕倾斜的能力，电动运动和视频通话比以前更好。亚马逊表示，此主题跟踪跨组调用，Skype，并将在支持此出现时使用缩放。这种效果是微妙的，但通常会试图让你在视野中的中心和大致相同的大小（从头到你的腰部到腰部），即使你远离相机。
 您也可以从Alexa应用程序中挖掘相机的Feed，无论您在互联网连接，都将其转换为Makeshift Home安全摄像头。您甚至可以使用电动基地远程绕过房间周围的相机。 Google的Nest Hub Max Smart Display具有类似的安全摄像头功能（减去机动运动），亚马逊表示计划将其带到较旧的Echo Show设备。您还可以使用相机的人员检测能力来触发通过Alexa应用程序的例程，例如用于自动转动或控制其他智能家用小工具。
   但是超越刚刚移动屏幕，所以它面向你最好，实际使用回声显示10的经验并不多于先前版本。这仍然是一种声音第一体验 - 你说“alexa”，要求它做点什么，它做到了。亚马逊已经向软件推出了一些小调整，因此屏幕有时会向您展示比曾经的信息更多，但在大多数情况下，它并不像在练习中使用的变革性，因为它看起来很糟糕。
 运动也使得展示以奇怪的方式行事。只要显示10正在播放某些东西，才能触摸屏幕或使用顶部边缘上的音量按钮，因为相机的视图很容易被阻止，并且节目将垂直蠕动，以便再次尝试使用框架。如果您确实成功地触摸屏幕或在移动时调整倾斜角度，则该展会有时会抱怨障碍物是障碍，直到您再次切断功能，然后再次恢复功能。亚马逊表示，这两件事都是由设计的，至少现在。
   尽管亚马逊声称回声显示10总是在视野中，但是当您需要时，现实是围绕一个固定点枢转的屏幕并不真正使得在烹饪时更容易使用或主动地移动到房间时更容易使用。当然，当从冰箱到柜台时，它可能会面对我一点，但是从倾斜角度看到屏幕上的屏幕上并不难。直到回声显示10实际上可以从它的位置起床，然后移动 - 甚至浮动，甚至浮动，仍然需要你走到它来互动或真正看到它试图向你展示什么。
 尽管如此，在从厨房到餐厅的情况下，我的运动仍然存在奇怪和令人不安的事情。如果我走得太快，但如果我超出其（令人惊讶的慷慨）的范围，但大多数情况下，屏幕上拍摄了一些东西，屏幕，屏幕就会失去我的追踪在我的方向，用一只眼睛看着我。它需要习惯，我不确定我还在吗？ 
得益于新的三扬声器系统，Echo Show 10确实听起来不错听音乐。像大多数其他Echo装置一样，尽管低音往往会变得蓬勃，但它可以轻松充满整个房间。不过，在Alexa应用程序中对EQ进行快速调整可轻松解决这一问题。
 确保所有扬声器始终面对您的技巧给我留下了深刻的印象-声音质量在没有准确指向我的方向时和指向我的方向之间并没有太大差异。
 亚马逊从竞争对手那里借来了一些功能来改善显示效果。最值得一提的是，它具有将屏幕的颜色调整为适合房间照明的能力，从而可以改善照片在屏幕上的显示方式。这个技巧不如Google Nest Hub好，但是比以前更好。将Echo Show 10用作数码相框的最大限制仍然是，您必须将图像保存在Amazon的Photos服务中或上传到Facebook，而我个人都不用这些东西。
 自大约三年前发布10英寸Echo Show的最后一次迭代以来，亚马逊增加了您可以在屏幕上观看的视频服务的数量。最近新增的最大功能是Netflix，但您也可以在其上观看Hulu或Amazon Prime视频。令人遗憾的是，尽管亚马逊在其Fire TV设备上支持其中的大多数服务，但仍无法访问其他服务，包括HBO Max，Apple TV Plus，孔雀，YouTube TV等。观看YouTube视频也仍然是一种可笑的糟糕体验，包括使用语音命令启动Echo Show 10的内置网络浏览器，然后手动搜索您要观看的视频。仍然无法将视频从手机投射到Echo Show 10，这仍然是一个令人讨厌的限制。
  我在厨房里住了三年的Echo Show，尽管我发现它的功能有限，但我和我的家人确实从中获得了很多价值。我们通过它管理共享的食品杂货清单，从中获取天气和新闻报道，在其上听来自Spotify的音乐，通过它控制智能家居小工具，并在心情变化时要求它提供随机琐事的答案。
 我曾希望新的Echo Show具有新的旋转屏幕和跟踪功能，可以为您带来更多的体验。但在大多数情况下，新的Echo Show在我们使用的所有功能上都没有比以前的型号好很多。更新后的模型在视频通话方面有了显着改善，但是正如我之前所说，这并不是我们在家中使用Echo Show的地方。
 那么，Echo Show 10的真正价值在于它的简洁。这是我们以前从未见过的智能显示屏上的一个新功能，而且如果一开始有点尴尬，可以观看它的工作，这很有趣。但是我认为这不是必需的，并且如果您有以前的Echo Show，则升级不会带来太多好处。 （如果您确实需要相机快门，则可以用一些胶带来做很多事情。） 
我对亚马逊的下一步发展感到更加兴奋-毕竟，有关该公司正在开发基于Alexa的机器人的传闻已经存在多年了。 也许我将等待Alexa的下一步行动。
